module_mood_contract: learning through contrast — 'I can feel what packaging adds now; I couldn't have read it as a lecture'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named and honoured.
Epistemic unease, not punitive unease.
Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea: 'Read the un-packaged Module 4 run through three failure-mode lenses, build the validation…' — diagnose → build → re-send is the positive discipline; failure modes are the diagnostic material, not the frame.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity check — no front-running of downstream teaching moments.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Strategy doc M5 § 'delivery shape': closing lecture (what-packaging-is.md) names Ronacher's three-pattern AFTER exercise + Debrief. File places the closing lecture link after the re-send prompt — correct ordering. Ronacher attribution withheld through Phases 1–4 per maintainer block 'Closing lecture pre-empt' push-back note.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts bullets hit: 90%-correct (implicit — 'no benchmark told you what went wrong; the run did'); compounding ('Practice beats external proof' breadcrumb); mirror/self-awareness ('You read the failure in your own artefact, named the three modes from what you saw'); non-agentic-default ('Same task run twice with packaging as the only changed variable IS the lesson'); capture ('Your verifier is your first eval. Module 6 gives evals their full weight as team infrastructure').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs, not standalone section.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bullet 5 uses the canonical breadcrumb pattern: '**Practice beats external proof.**' — bolded maxim at end of a tactical bullet. Others stay as tactical bullets without breadcrumb notation, which is within bounds (1–3 breadcrumbs max).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands — all phase scores 8+.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase scores: [8, 9, 8, 9]. Close: 9. No phase below 8.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M5 contrast does not pre-empt M6's practitioner-fluency teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts line 6: 'Your verifier is your first eval. Module 6 gives evals their full weight as team infrastructure.' — explicitly defers the full weight of evals to M6. Bridge: 'Subtler misses, subtler drift. Your verifier gets its full name as an eval, and the team kit takes its first inherited skill.' M5 does not resolve the eval-as-infrastructure arc; it names the starting point.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — contrast between un-packaged and packaged run fires reliably across persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The teaching moment (felt contrast from two runs on the same task) is structurally forced: the exercise builds the packaging BEFORE the re-send prompt; the re-send is the same task with packaging as the only changed variable. No persona can skip this — the re-send prompt is fenced and load-bearing. Phase 2 mood score 9 in trace confirms the moment fires.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — file leads with or honours the strategy doc's M5 Big Idea.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea in file: 'Read the un-packaged Module 4 run through three failure-mode lenses, build the validation that would have caught each, assemble the reference and plan.md, and re-send the same task packaged. The contrast is the lesson.' Strategy doc M5: 'The pedagogy is contrast, not prescription.' Direct match.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Module-to-module arc — M5 close hands off to M6 open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bridge: 'Module 6 reads the packaged run. Subtler misses, subtler drift. Your verifier gets its full name as an eval, and the team kit takes its first inherited skill.' Strategy doc M6 mood: practitioner fluency — 'I know how to test, I know how to learn, I know how to encode.' Handoff is clean and forward-loaded without spoiling M6.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text. No authored conversations, pause cues, or 'discuss with...' sentences.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Hard-grep check: no 'share with your neighbor', 'sitting next to you', 'tell the room', 'take 90 seconds', 'compare with the person next to you' or equivalent forms found in student-facing body. The only cue about pace ('The laptop stays awake and plugged in while it runs') is about the artifact-running mechanic, not cohort-management.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Both fenced prompts in the module file carry the forcing functions. Worktree-setup prompt: the cp commands + confirmation step are inside the fence. Re-send prompt: 'run the verifier per plan.md cadence; don't paper over failures; if you get stuck, write into RUN-NOTES.md' are inside the fence. Body prose around the re-send prompt ('The laptop stays awake…') is about artifact mechanics, not pedagogy. One optional refinement ('Ask Claude to re-run the same Module 4 task using the reference, plan.md, and verifier you just built.') precedes the fenced prompt as a body-prose lead-in — this is acceptable as an optional body framing before the fenced block, not a load-bearing forcing function displaced from the fence.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Closing lecture (what-packaging-is.md) is placed after exercise + Debrief + re-send prompt — correctly a closer. The two opening lectures (reading-the-return.md, learning-through-contrast.md) are openers/armers, not meta-frame lectures. Strategy doc confirms this placement. The pre-read (reading-the-return.md) plants the question; learning-through-contrast.md arms the student for diagnosis — neither names the three-pattern. What-packaging-is.md names the pattern from felt evidence: correctly a closer.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Minimal lecturing before contrast-mood exercises.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Per strategy doc: M5 opener lecture (learning-through-contrast.md) carries 'spirit + diagnostic lenses + one operational primitive (manual /compact at ~60%) + a direct-descriptive FAQ'. The module page includes two lecture links before the exercise; both serve as armers, not framers. Dosage is within the contrast-mood cap.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — three observable Monday-morning outcomes in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block does not contain an explicit 'leap test' section, BUT the module-level maintainer block contains 'Mood target', 'Watch-fors', 'Decision points', 'Push-back moves', 'Plug points', and 'Frameworks riffed on' — all load-bearing. The leap test requirement (new 2026-05-03) was not yet present in this file's audit cycle (Quality tag: draft 2026-04-30). Story judge scores this N/A for blocking since it is a new rule; flagging as TODO for next audit cycle.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a 'Leap test' section to the maintainer block naming three observable Monday-morning outcomes — e.g., 'opens a worktree from the M4 branch and diagnoses the run using three named lenses', 'ships a verifier shaped against their dominant failure mode', 'sends off the same task packaged with reference + plan.md + verifier and records the contrast'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch for every forcing function.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module file's maintainer block has an extensive 'Push-back moves' section naming per-phase failure modes and escape hatches: 'Phase 1 diagnosis-as-blame' + Nerd line; 'Phase 1 over-diagnosis' + Nerd line; 'Phase 2 prescription-skip' + Nerd line; 'Phase 3 verifier-shape mismatch' + Nerd line; 'Phase 4 reference-as-rewrite' + Nerd line; 'Debrief re-send anxiety' + Nerd line; 'Closing lecture pre-empt' + warning. Decision-points pacing section adds per-phase time diagnostics. Coverage is complete against the two module-level fenced prompts and the exercise phases referenced.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts — every produced file is a contract row in the maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module produces: worktree at ../<repo>-m5 (created in setup prompt, named in body prose); CLAUDE.local.md copy (cp command in prompt); re-send run artefacts (RUN-NOTES.md named in fenced prompt). Strategy doc M5 detail confirms: worktree-fork is the contract to M6 (M6 reads the packaged run from this worktree). Maintainer block delivery-architecture section states 'The re-send at Debrief happens in the same Claude Code session' and 'No new session, no scheduled agent, no cloud runner.' Artefact contracts are implicit in the setup prompt and delivery-architecture note; they are not formatted as a dedicated contract-rows table. Rule 46 originated 2026-05-03 — flagging as TODO for next audit cycle to add an explicit artefact-contracts section.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add an 'Artefact contracts' section to the maintainer block listing: worktree path ../<repo>-m5 on branch m5/<slug> (produced by setup prompt, consumed by M6); reference artefact + plan.md at paths written in this session (produced by exercise Phase 4 / module Debrief, consumed by re-send prompt); RUN-NOTES.md (produced by re-send, consumed by M6 analysis).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Worktree-setup prompt: lead-in is the body paragraph beginning 'Before opening the Module 5 session, fork the Module 4 starting SHA…' — semantic, under 20 words effective summary ('fork M4 SHA into sibling worktree, copy personal files across'). Re-send prompt: lead-in is 'Ask Claude to re-run the same Module 4 task using the reference, plan.md, and verifier you just built.' — exactly the canonical one-sentence action lead-in with command verb.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Worktree prompt uses `<repo-name>` as a pattern label embedded in shell strings — this is a template placeholder convention familiar to terminal-comfortable AE101 engineers. However it IS a form of placeholder inside a fenced block. The prompt tells Claude to 'find the branch starting with m4/' (Claude self-discovers) and the cp commands use `<repo-name>` as a fill-in. This is borderline but PASS: the prompt instructs Claude to self-discover the branch and the cp commands are shown as illustrative — Claude would write the actual paths, not the engineer. The re-send prompt has no placeholders ('the paths we wrote in this session' is a scrollback back-reference, rule 5 compliant).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Both prompts follow the canonical shape: action lead-in → **Prompt** *(Claude Code)* → blank line → fenced block. No blockquote wrapping, no copy-prefix, no end-of-prompt closer.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Re-send prompt: 'The reference, plan.md, and verifier are at the paths we wrote in this session.' — scrollback back-reference. Worktree prompt produces named paths via Claude ('Tell me the worktree path'). The chain is: worktree-setup → exercise (external file) → re-send. Cross-file chain is via the exercise file, which is separate scope.
